Overview

Onboarding Overview Pre-Onboarding Checklist

Phase 1 — Admin Setup Phase 2 — Site Manager Phase 3 — Caretaker Phase 4 — First Week Live Phase 5 — Ongoing Support

Onboarding Guide

Version 1.0 — March 2026 · For UK Administrators and Site Managers responsible for onboarding

Onboarding Overview

Onboarding follows a top-down approach: UK admins configure the system first, then train site managers, who then onboard caretakers on the ground.

Week 1
Admin Setup
Create users, set budgets, exchange rates
➔
Week 2
Site Manager Onboarding
Admin login, review views, team planning
➔
Week 3+
Caretaker Onboarding
First message, practice run, go live

Total onboarding time per site: 1–2 weeks from admin setup to live expenses.

Pre-Onboarding Checklist

System Prerequisites

  • Django application is deployed and accessible at the production URL
  • Celery worker is running on the background processing server
  • PostgreSQL database is running with seed data loaded
  • Redis is running (for message deduplication)

Messaging Channel Prerequisites

For WhatsApp (Meta Cloud API):

  • Meta Developer account and Meta Business account created
  • Meta App with WhatsApp product configured
  • Webhook URL set pointing to /webhooks/whatsapp/
  • "messages" webhook field subscribed
  • Permanent access token generated via System User
  • Test message sent and received successfully

For Telegram:

  • Telegram bot created via @BotFather
  • Bot token configured in .env
  • Webhook registered pointing to /webhooks/telegram/
  • Test /start command works

Data Prerequisites

  • Organisation "City Centre Dawah" exists (created by seed_data)
  • All three sites exist: Kampala, Banjul, Indonesia
  • Budget categories are loaded (10 categories)
  • Exchange rates are current for UGX, GMD, IDR
  • Annual budgets are set for the current financial year

Phase 1 — Admin Setup (UK Side)

Who: UK Administrator · Time: 30–60 minutes per site

Step 1: Create the Site Manager Account

1
Go to Core → Users → Add User
2
Fill in username, password, name, email, organisation, site, role (site_manager), phone, Telegram username
3
Set Staff status = Checked (required for Admin access). Do NOT set Superuser status.
4
Click Save

Step 2: Create Caretaker Accounts

For each caretaker:

FieldValueNotes
UsernameUnique namee.g. caretaker_john_kampala
OrganisationCity Centre Dawah
SiteTheir orphanageMust match the site manager's site
Rolecaretaker
PhoneWhatsApp numberCritical — exact international format
Telegram usernameTheir @ nameCritical — must match actual username

Step 3: Set Annual Budgets

For each category at the site, go to Expenses → Site budgets → Add and enter Site, Category, Financial year, and Annual amount (GBP).

CategorySet a budget?Notes
FoodYesTypically the largest expense
SalariesYesPredictable, should match payroll
UtilitiesYesRelatively stable
MedicalYesImportant to track, can spike
ClothingYesSeasonal
EducationYesTerm-based
MaintenanceYesPrevents overspend on ad-hoc repairs
TransportationYesCan creep up
RenovationsOptionalMay be project-based
ContingencyOptionalBy definition unpredictable

Step 4: Verify Exchange Rates

Check Expenses → Exchange rates for current rates. Update weekly or monthly.

Step 5: Send Test Message

1
Register your own phone number as a user
2
Send: Food 1000 test expense please delete
3
Verify confirmation reply and check the expense in Admin
4
Delete the test expense

Phase 2 — Site Manager Onboarding

Who: UK Administrator (via video call) · Time: 30–45 minutes per site manager

Session Agenda

TopicTimeCover
Introduction5 minSystem purpose, high-level flow
Logging In5 minAdmin URL, login process, Google SSO
Expense List10 minFilters, budget warning badges, expense details
Budget vs Actual10 minColumns explained, replaces Excel summary
Message Pipeline5 minMessage logs, automatic processing, statuses
Responsibilities5 minContact details, issue reporting, budget monitoring
Questions & Practice5 minFree exploration, Q&A

Post-Session Tasks

  • Log into admin independently
  • Find and filter expenses for their site
  • Review the budget vs actual view
  • Confirm all caretaker contact details are correct
  • Report any discrepancies to UK admin

Phase 3 — Caretaker Onboarding

Who: Site Manager (in person) · Time: 15–20 minutes per caretaker

Before the Session

  • Confirm the caretaker's phone number is registered
  • Confirm their Telegram username is registered
  • Have the bot's WhatsApp number or Telegram bot name ready
  • Print the Quick Reference Card (see below)

Session Steps

1
Save the Bot Contact (2 min) — Give the caretaker the bot number/username, have them save it and open a chat
2
Explain the Format (5 min) — Write on a board: Category Amount Description with examples
3
Practice Message (5 min) — Have them send Food 1000 onboarding test and read the confirmation together
4
Receipt Photo (3 min) — Practise attaching a photo with a caption expense
5
Discuss Rules (5 min) — One expense per message, send as you spend, keep receipts, check replies, budget warnings
6
Hand Out Reference Card
After onboarding Delete all test expenses from the system before going live.

Phase 4 — First Week Live

Day 1–2: Active Monitoring

UK Admin:

  • Check incoming expenses every few hours
  • Verify amounts are reasonable
  • Confirm exchange rates are producing sensible GBP amounts
  • Look for formatting errors in message logs

Site Manager:

  • Be available to help caretakers with questions
  • Remind caretakers to use the bot instead of paper
  • Check all caretakers have sent at least one real expense

Day 3–5: Light Monitoring

  • First batch review — mark legitimate expenses as "Reviewed"
  • Query any suspicious expenses
  • Check budget vs actual — are numbers sensible?

End of Week 1: Review

  • Are all caretakers using the system?
  • Are the categories appropriate?
  • Are the budgets realistic?
  • Are exchange rates accurate?
  • Any technical issues?

Phase 5 — Ongoing Support

Monthly Tasks

TaskWho
Update exchange ratesUK Admin (weekly or monthly)
Review and approve expensesUK Admin (weekly)
Check budget vs actualUK Admin + Site Manager
Verify all caretakers are activeSite Manager

Quarterly Tasks

TaskWho
Review budget allocationsUK Admin
Deactivate unused categoriesUK Admin
Review audit logs for anomaliesUK Admin
Refresh caretaker training if neededSite Manager

Common Support Requests

RequestAction
"My message isn't working"Check phone/username registration, verify format
"I sent the wrong amount"Admin can edit or flag as Queried
"New caretaker joined"Create user, register contacts, run onboarding
"Caretaker left"Deactivate user (don't delete)
"Need a new category"Create under Budget Categories, inform all caretakers
"Budget needs changing"Edit the budget record for that site/category/year

Onboarding a New Site

1
Create the Site — Core → Sites → Add (name, country, city, currency)
2
Add Exchange Rate — Expenses → Exchange rates → Add
3
Set Up Budgets — Create a Site budget for each category
4
Create Users — Site manager + all caretaker accounts
5
Configure Messaging — Same bot works for all sites (identified by phone/username)
6
Run Onboarding — Follow Phases 2–4 above

Quick Reference Card

Print and laminate for each caretaker:

CCD EXPENSE BOT — QUICK REFERENCE

How to send an expense:

Category  Amount  Description

Food      50000   rice Kalerwe
Medical   25000   clinic visit
Utilities 15000   electricity
Education 100000  school fees term 2
Clothing  30000   uniforms

Categories:

Food
Salaries
Utilities
Medical
Clothing
Education
Maintenance
Transportation
Renovations
Contingency

Rules:

  • One expense per message
  • Amount in local currency (no symbols)
  • Attach receipt photo when possible
  • Always check the reply for confirmation
  • If you see a budget warning, tell your site manager

Problems? Contact your site manager

Checklist Templates

Per-Site Onboarding Checklist

SITE: ______________   DATE: ______________

Phase 1 — Admin Setup (UK)

  • Site exists in system
  • Exchange rate set for local currency
  • Site budgets created for all categories
  • Site manager account created (staff status = true)
  • All caretaker accounts created
  • All phone numbers in international format
  • All Telegram usernames entered
  • Test message sent and confirmed working

Phase 2 — Site Manager Onboarding

  • Site manager logged in successfully
  • Walked through expense list and filters
  • Walked through budget vs actual view
  • Shown message logs
  • Responsibilities discussed and understood

Phase 3 — Caretaker Onboarding

  • Bot contact saved
  • Format explained
  • Practice message sent and confirmed
  • Receipt photo tested
  • Rules discussed
  • Reference card given

Phase 4 — First Week Live

  • Day 1-2: Active monitoring complete
  • Day 3-5: First batch review done
  • End of week: Review meeting held
  • All test expenses deleted
  • All caretakers submitting real expenses
{% endblock %}
